content.problemStatement:
Chef loves single-colored squares, so he got a rectangular board with size N√óMN \times MN√óM consisting only of black cells and white cells. He calculates the cost of the board in the following manner:


Let's denote the number of ways to choose k2k^2k2 white cells which form a square with edge length kkk (and edges parallel to the borders of the board) by WkW_kWk‚Äã. For example, a 2√ó32 \times 32√ó3 board consisting of 6 white cells and no black cells has W1=6W_1=6W1‚Äã=6, W2=2W_2=2W2‚Äã=2 and W3=0W_3=0W3‚Äã=0.
Similarly, let's denote the number of ways to choose k2k^2k2 black cells forming a square by BkB_kBk‚Äã.
Chef chose two sequences of non-negative integers CWC_WCW‚Äã and CBC_BCB‚Äã, each with length min(N,M)\mathrm{min}(N, M)min(N,M). The cost of the board is defined as
‚àëi=1min(N,M)CB,i‚ãÖBi+CW,i‚ãÖWi‚Äâ.\sum_{i=1}^{\mathrm{min}(N, M)} C_{B, i} \cdot B_i + C_{W, i} \cdot W_i\,.‚àëi=1min(N,M)‚ÄãCB,i‚Äã‚ãÖBi‚Äã+CW,i‚Äã‚ãÖWi‚Äã.
However, Chef accidentally spilled some sauce on the board while cooking, so the colors of some cells cannot be determined. Now he wants to repaint each of these cells using black or white paint. What is the maximum achievable cost of the resulting board? Note that he may paint different cells using different colors.
constraints: each string contains only characters '0', '1' and '?'
0‚â§CW,i‚â§1080 \le C_{W, i} \le 10^80‚â§CW,i‚Äã‚â§108 for each valid iii
0‚â§CB,i‚â§1080 \le C_{B, i} \le 10^80‚â§CB,i‚Äã‚â§108 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), the iii-th of these lines contains a string with length MMM describing the iii-th row of the board ‚Äî the character '0' stands for a white cell, '1' stands for a black cell and '?' stands for a cell with unknown color.
The following line contains K=min(N,M)K=\mathrm{min}(N, M)K=min(N,M) space-separated integers CW,1,CW,2,‚Ä¶,CW,KC_{W, 1}, C_{W, 2}, \dots, C_{W, K}CW,1‚Äã,CW,2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CW,K‚Äã.
The last line contains K=min(N,M)K=\mathrm{min}(N, M)K=min(N,M) space-separated integers CB,1,CB,2,‚Ä¶,CB,KC_{B, 1}, C_{B, 2}, \dots, C_{B, K}CB,1‚Äã,CB,2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CB,K‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the maximum possible cost.

Explanation:
